attack_paths.name: Server → Internal Services
attack_paths.description: Multiple server misconfigs increase attack surface

patches.instructions: Open the printed config snippet and add the appropriate block to your Flask app config or Django settings.py. These settings require HTTPS to be active — enabling Secure cookies on an HTTP-only site will break sessions.
For Flask: install flask-wtf and initialise CSRFProtect. For Django: ensure CsrfViewMiddleware is in MIDDLEWARE and {% csrf_token %} is in all POST forms.

Either add authentication to admin routes in your application code, or block them entirely at the nginx level using the provided location block. The nginx approach is faster to apply and does not require app restart.
For Flask: install Flask-Limiter and apply limits to sensitive routes. For production, use Redis as storage_uri instead of memory://. Nginx-level rate limiting is recommended as an additional layer.
patches.verification: for i in $(seq 1 20); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' https://your-app.com/api/; done

patches.instructions: Add the validate_password function to your user registration and password change routes.
patches.verification: Test registration with a weak password like '123' — it should return a 400 error.

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-hsts.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-hsts.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./fix-hsts.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: ./fix-hsts.sh --apply
6. The script will automatically backup original configs, test nginx configuration, and reload nginx if successful

1. Download and save this script as 'WS-SEC-001.sh'
2. Make it executable: chmod +x WS-SEC-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: ./WS-SEC-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./WS-SEC-001.sh --apply
6. Verify with the verification command below
Add these directives to your nginx HTTPS server block. After applying, verify with SSL Labs: https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/analyze.html?d=your-domain.com

1. Save this script as WS-SRV-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x WS-SRV-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./WS-SRV-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./WS-SRV-001.sh --apply
6. Verify the fix using the verification command
patches.verification: curl -I http://localhost 2>/dev/null | grep -i server || echo 'Server header hidden successfully'

patches.instructions: Add these directives to your nginx server block. Adjust client_max_body_size to match your application's upload requirements.

1. Save this script as HOST-SSH-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-SSH-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: sudo ./HOST-SSH-001.sh --dry-run
4. Review the changes that would be made
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./HOST-SSH-001.sh --apply
6. Verify SSH service is running: sudo systemctl status ssh
patches.verification: grep '^PermitRootLogin no' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && sudo systemctl is-active ssh

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script as HOST-FW-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-FW-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./HOST-FW-001.sh
4. Review the planned changes
5. Apply the fix: ./HOST-FW-001.sh --apply
6. Verify SSH access still works before closing your current session

1. Make the script executable: chmod +x HOST-SVC-001.sh
2. Run in dry-run mode first to see what would be changed: ./HOST-SVC-001.sh
3. Review the output carefully to ensure no critical services would be disabled
4. Apply the changes: ./HOST-SVC-001.sh --apply
5. Verify system functionality after applying changes
6. If any issues occur, restore services from the backup directory shown in output
